name: Tales of Klodan
short_description: Sent to Klodan as a royal envoy, the youngest prince of Fescilia expects diplomacy—not a land of fear and silence. With a trusted friend at his side, he is drawn into court intrigues and an encounter with a masked stranger. Six paths lie ahead, each leading to a different fate. Which will he choose?
detailed_description:
In this episodic visual novel, your choices shape the story, determine romantic paths, and lead to one of eight possible endings.

ABOUT THIS RELEASE

• This is an episodic visual novel.

• The game is purchased once and includes all current and future chapters.

• The current release includes the first chapter of the story.

• Romance routes and multiple endings are introduced gradually in later chapters.

STORY OVERVIEW

The youngest prince of Fescilia is sent north on a mission he never asked for — and was never truly prepared for. Chosen more for convenience than experience, he is dispatched to Klodan, a harsh mountain kingdom buried under snow and old fears. In Fescilia, Klodan is the subject of unsettling tales, whispered rather than told aloud. Still, this is meant to be a diplomatic journey: peace talks, trade agreements, polite words exchanged across a table.

Officially, he travels as an envoy of the crown. Unofficially, he is expendable.

By his side is Leo — his personal guard and closest friend, the one constant he trusts more than court etiquette or carefully memorized speeches.

Klodan greets them with cold stone, colder smiles, and an atmosphere thick with things left unsaid. The people are courteous, but guarded. The castles are grand, yet oppressive. Beneath the surface lie old agreements, broken promises, and political games far more dangerous than the prince anticipated.

And then there is the masked stranger — a figure wearing the face of a wolf. Elusive, unsettling, and impossible to read, he appears at the worst possible moment, blurring the line between ally and threat.

The prince came to negotiate peace. Instead, he finds himself caught in a web of intrigue, deception, and choices that will shape far more than a single treaty.

What awaits him in Klodan — alliance, betrayal, or something far darker — depends on the paths he dares to take.

GAME FEATURES

• 35+ unique locations across the snowbound kingdom of Klodan.

• 9 endings shaped by your choices and actions (across all chapters).

• 2 romanceable characters with deep, intertwining stories.

• A web of secrets and intrigue waiting to be unraveled.

• Original atmospheric soundtrack enhancing the mystery.

• A hidden storyline for those who dare to look deeper.

• Branching story paths and choices designed for replayability.

reviews.review_text:
My girlfriend made this. Be nice


Ps. some curator named "Based propaganda detector" said "created by homosexual pedophiles" which is untrue. I can assure you - I have not seen this curator's parents nowhere near the creation process

[b]💬 Supportive Caveat[/b]

I received this game for free through the Steam Curator Connect program, but that does not influence my review. This is also an episodic release, and at the time of review only Chapter 1 is available, with future chapters confirmed to be included in the base purchase price. What is here is clearly a partial experience, but it is priced cheaply enough that the scope feels fair.

The developer also discloses that AI was used to initially generate some visual assets, which were then processed and completed by humans. Personally, I did not find the visuals off-putting or sloppy, but I know some players will care about that disclosure much more than I do.


[b]🎮 Gameplay & Genre[/b]

Tales of Klodan is a choice-driven fantasy visual novel with light dating sim elements. This first chapter took me just under two hours and is mostly focused on setup, lore, and establishing the main cast rather than delivering major route divergence. Dialogue choices appear which will seemingly shape the upcoming chapters, and the store page promises multiple endings and deeper branching across future chapters, but for now this feels more like an opening act than a fully route-heavy VN. Mechanically, it is very straightforward, with the focus resting almost entirely on story, atmosphere, and character dynamics.


[b]🔥 M/M Content[/b]

This is where expectations matter. While it features male romantic routes, the tone leans much closer to BL-style fantasy romance than to a more explicitly M/M-forward experience. The protagonist, Adrian, is a twinkish young prince, and the two love interests introduced here are Léonar, his loyal twunk guard and close companion, and Agnar, a more muscular jock type. The romantic energy is soft, restrained, and very much in its early stages.

There is no nudity, which is completely fine, but the censorship choice is awkward. Instead of simply blurring or partially obscuring the groin area, the game uses Adrian’s shocked face as a censor overlay. It comes across as more jarring than effective. In a gay VN space, naked men are not exactly a shocking reveal, so this feels tonally mismatched with the rest of the presentation.


[b]🎨 Visuals & Style[/b]

The art is pleasant, with attractive character sprites and backgrounds that support the cold, snowy fantasy setting well. Despite the AI disclosure, the visuals feel intentional and cohesive rather than cheap or incoherent. The music is a highlight, adding a strong sense of atmosphere that helps carry the slower, lore-heavy pacing of this opening chapter. The only real visual misstep is the censorship device, which stands out in a way the rest of the presentation does not.


[b]🗣️ Tone & Writing[/b]

The writing leans heavily into worldbuilding, political setup, and fantasy lore, so the story moves a bit slowly at first. That said, I enjoyed it overall. The pacing makes sense for a first chapter that is laying groundwork, even if it means sacrificing some immediate emotional payoff.

The character dynamics help a lot. Adrian and Léonar already have a sweet, believable bond, and Agnar’s introduction adds intrigue without feeling forced. The tone is earnest and gentle, with more of a soft romantic fantasy feel than anything overtly erotic or high-intensity.


[b]👍 Best Thing[/b]

The sweet character dynamics and overall atmosphere give this first chapter a solid emotional foundation.


[b]👎 Worst Thing[/b]

The shocked-face censorship is distracting and noticeably less effective than simpler alternatives would have been.


[b]⭐ Final Verdict[/b]

This is a modest but appealing first chapter for a fantasy BL-style gay visual novel. It is slow, light on payoff, and still mostly in setup mode, but the art, music, and character work make it easy to enjoy. For $2.99, it is a worthwhile opening chapter and an easy way to support a new dev team with some promise.



I also curate a Steam page reviewing M/M games if you’re looking for more titles in this space: https://store.steampowered.com/curator/45790038/
A BL visual novel. I quite like the first episode.
